unit.name: Pretraining scale and post-training motivation
unit.description: Motivates post-training from the scale and emergent behavior of pretrained LLMs.
unit.summary: The lecture starts by saying the goal is to understand how pretrained models become ChatGPT-like systems through prompting, instruction fine-tuning, and RLHF [ts=32s] [ts=40s]. It reviews scaling laws: compute has moved beyond older 10^24 FLOP plots toward more than 10^26 FLOPs, and data has scaled to roughly 15 trillion tokens for recent Llama 3 models [ts=64s] [ts=121s]. Although pretraining optimizes next-token prediction, the instructor argues it learns more than syntax and facts: examples suggest models can represent agents, beliefs, actions, math, code, and medical-style behavior [ts=148s] [ts=304s]. This motivates post-training as the step that turns a powerful text predictor into a usable assistant [ts=391s].

key_points.text: Post-training is framed as the bridge from pretrained models to ChatGPT-like assistants.
Recent pretraining scale is described as above 10^26 FLOPs and about 15T tokens for Llama 3-scale models.
Next-token prediction can learn knowledge, syntax, semantics, and richer models of agents and beliefs.
The central question is how to transform a simple text predictor into ChatGPT-like behavior.

Although pretraining optimizes next-token prediction, the instructor argues it learns more than syntax and facts: examples suggest models can represent agents, beliefs, actions, math, code, and medical-style behavior [ts=148s] [ts=304s]. This motivates post-training as the step that turns a powerful text predictor into a usable assistant [ts=391s].

kp_catalog.name: Pretraining scaling and compute-data tradeoffs
kp_catalog.description: Large-scale pretraining quality depends jointly on model size, training data, and compute budget, with capabilities improving as those resources scale. This scaling view also appears in vision-language systems such as CLIP, where larger models and more image-text data improve performance.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because pretraining scaling and compute-data tradeoffs clarifies large-scale pretraining quality depends jointly on model size, training data, and compute budget, with capabilities improving as those resources scale, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices while importance labels split between high and medium.

kp_catalog.name: InstructGPT and ChatGPT post-training pipeline
kp_catalog.description: The practical assistant-building pipeline and its remaining preference-alignment risks.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because instructGPT and ChatGPT post-training pipeline synthesizes the practical assistant-building pipeline and its remaining preference-alignment risks into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.
